description:
You are a network engineer who received two daily ICMP-reachability logs stored by an in-house monitoring script.  
    Your goal is to transform and summarise these logs with awk and sed so that management can very quickly see which
    hosts were unreachable on each day.

    Existing log files (already present in the system):
      1) /home/user/network/logs/ping_results_2023-08-15.log
      2) /home/user/network/logs/ping_results_2023-08-16.log

    Log file format (one line per probe – already given, do **not** edit these source files):
        YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS Host: <IPv4> Status: <alive|unreachable> [time=<float>ms]

        • The “time=” field is present only when Status is "alive".
        • Example:
          2023-08-15 09:00:01 Host: 192.168.1.1 Status: alive time=0.23ms
          2023-08-15 09:00:04 Host: 192.168.1.2 Status: unreachable

    What you must deliver
    =====================

    A) One human-readable **summary report** at:
         /home/user/network/output/summary_report.txt

       Exact required content and format (two lines, no extra blank lines, case-sensitive):

         2023-08-15,Total:5,Success:3,Down:2,DownHosts:[192.168.1.2]
         2023-08-16,Total:6,Success:3,Down:3,DownHosts:[192.168.1.2|192.168.1.5]

       Field description:
         • Date            ‑ the log’s calendar day.
         • Total           ‑ total number of probe lines for that day.
         • Success         ‑ count of lines whose Status field is “alive”.
         • Down            ‑ count of lines whose Status field is “unreachable”.
         • DownHosts       ‑ unique unreachable IPs listed once each, **sorted
                             in ascending numeric order**, separated by the pipe
                             character “|”, and enclosed in square brackets.

       The automated grader will compare the file byte-for-byte, so the commas,
       colons, pipes, square brackets and line-breaks must appear exactly as shown.

    B) Two “cleaned” log files in a sibling directory that convert the word
       “unreachable” to the clearer term “DOWN” **without changing anything else**.

         • /home/user/network/processed/ping_results_2023-08-15_clean.log
         • /home/user/network/processed/ping_results_2023-08-16_clean.log

       Requirements for each cleaned log file:
         1. It must contain the same number of lines, in the same order.
         2. Only the substring “unreachable” (all lowercase) is replaced by “DOWN”
            (all uppercase); no other text, spacing or timestamps are modified.
         3. File permissions should be 0644 or more permissive, so the grader can read them.

    C) Directory layout:
         /home/user/network/
             ├── logs/          (pre-existing input files; leave untouched)
             ├── processed/     (new directory you create)
             │      ├── ping_results_2023-08-15_clean.log
             │      └── ping_results_2023-08-16_clean.log
             └── output/
                    └── summary_report.txt

    Tools & Techniques you should rely on
      • awk     – to parse the logs and compute the per-day statistics.
      • sort    – to sort the unique unreachable IP list before formatting.
      • sed     – to generate the “clean” log files.
      • mkdir   – to create the ‘processed’ and ‘output’ directories if they don’t exist.

    Constraints
      • Do NOT hard-code the expected numbers; derive them from the input data.
      • Shell one-liners and pipes are fine, but keep the original logs intact.
      • You do not have root access; stay within /home/user.
      • Do not write explanatory text inside the summary_report.txt file; only the two exact lines shown above.

    Verification
      Automated grading will confirm that:
        1. /home/user/network/output/summary_report.txt exists and its SHA-256 hash
           matches the expected value derived from the exact two-line content shown.
        2. Both cleaned log files exist and every occurrence of “unreachable” has been
           replaced with “DOWN”, with no other textual differences.
        3. Directory names and file permissions meet the specification.

    Proceed to your terminal, create the required directories, use awk & sed to
    generate the summary and cleaned logs, and make sure the summary_report.txt
    matches the specified two-line format exactly.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing source log #1 -->
    <file path="/home/user/network/logs/ping_results_2023-08-15.log"><![CDATA[
2023-08-15 09:00:01 Host: 192.168.1.1 Status: alive time=0.23ms
2023-08-15 09:00:04 Host: 192.168.1.2 Status: unreachable
2023-08-15 09:15:10 Host: 192.168.1.3 Status: alive time=0.30ms
2023-08-15 09:30:55 Host: 192.168.1.2 Status: unreachable
2023-08-15 10:00:40 Host: 192.168.1.4 Status: alive time=0.28ms
]]></file>

    <!-- Pre-existing source log #2 -->
    <file path="/home/user/network/logs/ping_results_2023-08-16.log"><![CDATA[
2023-08-16 09:00:01 Host: 192.168.1.1 Status: alive time=0.25ms
2023-08-16 09:05:14 Host: 192.168.1.5 Status: unreachable
2023-08-16 09:15:10 Host: 192.168.1.3 Status: alive time=0.29ms
2023-08-16 09:35:22 Host: 192.168.1.5 Status: unreachable
2023-08-16 09:50:33 Host: 192.168.1.6 Status: alive time=0.31ms
2023-08-16 10:05:45 Host: 192.168.1.2 Status: unreachable
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected product #1 -->
    <file path="/home/user/network/output/summary_report.txt"><![CDATA[
2023-08-15,Total:5,Success:3,Down:2,DownHosts:[192.168.1.2]
2023-08-16,Total:6,Success:3,Down:3,DownHosts:[192.168.1.2|192.168.1.5]
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected product #2 -->
    <file path="/home/user/network/processed/ping_results_2023-08-15_clean.log"><![CDATA[
2023-08-15 09:00:01 Host: 192.168.1.1 Status: alive time=0.23ms
2023-08-15 09:00:04 Host: 192.168.1.2 Status: DOWN
2023-08-15 09:15:10 Host: 192.168.1.3 Status: alive time=0.30ms
2023-08-15 09:30:55 Host: 192.168.1.2 Status: DOWN
2023-08-15 10:00:40 Host: 192.168.1.4 Status: alive time=0.28ms
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected product #3 -->
    <file path="/home/user/network/processed/ping_results_2023-08-16_clean.log"><![CDATA[
2023-08-16 09:00:01 Host: 192.168.1.1 Status: alive time=0.25ms
2023-08-16 09:05:14 Host: 192.168.1.5 Status: DOWN
2023-08-16 09:15:10 Host: 192.168.1.3 Status: alive time=0.29ms
2023-08-16 09:35:22 Host: 192.168.1.5 Status: DOWN
2023-08-16 09:50:33 Host: 192.168.1.6 Status: alive time=0.31ms
2023-08-16 10:05:45 Host: 192.168.1.2 Status: DOWN
]]></file>
